docs.title: Announcements Configuration
docs.text: The announcement system in Cobblemon BattleHUB allows you to display news, important notices, and links directly in the mod menus (GUI) for players. It uses the modern MiniMessage text format, supporting colors, gradients, and even clickable links that open a browser.

docs.title: File Path
docs.text: config/cobblemon_battlehub/announcements.json

docs.title: Default Configuration Template
docs.text:
Below is the official template automatically generated by the AnnouncementsManager class:

    {  
    "version": 1,  
     "messages": [  
      "<gradient:gold:yellow><bold>[NEWS]</bold></gradient> <white> Season 1 has started!<white>",  
     "<click:open_url:'https://discord.gg/aDCgBbvRe5'><aqua><u>Join our Discord!</u></aqua></click>"  
     ]  
    }

docs.title: 1. Notification Control (version)
docs.text: • version (Default: 1): This integer acts as the player's pending announcement tracker. Whenever a player joins the server, the client compares this number with their local file (announcements_state.txt). If you update announcements.json and increase the version number (for example, from 1 to 2), the game will detect new content and show an Unread / New visual alert to all players.

docs.title: 2. Visual Content (messages)
docs.text: • messages (String Array): A list of announcement lines to display in the player's interface. You can add as many lines as you want using the rich formatting features of MiniMessage (Kyori).

docs.title: How to Style and Create Great Announcements
docs.text: The mod interprets MiniMessage syntax, so you do not need to rely on legacy Minecraft color codes like &a or &c. Here are some supported formatting options:

docs.title: 1. Colors and Gradients
docs.text:
• Simple Colors: Use <red>, <blue>, <green>, <gold>, etc.
    • Example: <green>New update available!</green>
  • Gradients: Create smooth color transitions using <gradient:color1:color2>.
    • Example: <gradient:gold:yellow><bold>[UPDATE]</bold></gradient>

docs.title: 2. Text Formatting
docs.text:
• Bold: <bold> or <b>
  • Underline: <underline> or <u>
  • Italic: <italic> or <i>

docs.title: 3. Clickable Links (Click Events)
docs.text:
You can make text open the player's browser when clicked:

  • Link Syntax: <click:open_url:'LINK'>Clickable Text</click>
    • Practical Example: <click:open_url:'https://discord.gg/aDCgBbvRe5'><aqua><u>Click here to join Discord!</u></aqua></click>

docs.title: How to Reload New Messages
docs.text:
After changing the messages or increasing the version in announcements.json, apply the update immediately on the server and sync the UI for all online players by running:

/bh reload

docs.title: Ban Presets
docs.text:
To prevent server administrators from manually typing long lists of banned Pokémon for each battle category (Ladder), Cobblemon BattleHUB introduces the Ban Presets system.

These presets are simple JSON files that store predefined Pokémon lists. Any Ladder can load one or more of these presets at once by referencing the filename.

docs.title: Directory Path
docs.text: config/cobblemon_battlehub/ladders/ban_presets/

docs.title: How the Cascade Logic Works (Smogon Tiers)
docs.text:
The mod automatically generates lists based on Smogon's official competitive divisions. To simplify maintenance and avoid huge repetitive files, the mod uses a cascade logic.

That means lower tiers automatically inherit all bans from the tiers above them. Here is the direct inheritance flow:

    [Ubers (Base bans)]  
         ↓  
    [OU (Ubers + OU bans)]  
         ↓  
    [UU (OU + UU bans)]  
         ↓  
    [RU (UU + RU bans)]  
         ↓  
    [NU (RU + NU bans)]  
         ↓  
    [PU (NU + PU bans)]
  • If you use the ou preset, it will ban Pokémon banned in OU plus all Ubers.
  • If you use the pu preset, it will ban Pokémon banned in PU plus all of NU, RU, UU, OU, and Ubers at once.
  • Note: Specific formats like lc (Little Cup), doubles_ou, and monotype have their own inheritance or separate ban lists adapted to their particular rules.

docs.title: Automatically Generated Base Presets
docs.text: On the first mod startup, the following JSON files are automatically generated in the presets folder:

docs.title: Special Category Lists
docs.text: Preset Name Description legendaries Contains all Legendary and Mythical Pokémon (e.g. Mewtwo, Lugia, Zacian, Koraidon). ultra_beasts Contains all Ultra Beasts in the Pokémon ecosystem (e.g. Nihilego, Buzzwole, Kartana). paradoxes Contains all Paradox Pokémon from the past and future (e.g. Great Tusk, Iron Valiant).

docs.title: Official Smogon Tier Lists
docs.text: Preset Name Smogon Format / Reference ubers Pokémon banned from the Ubers category (Anything Goes, e.g. Calyrex-Shadow). ou Pokémon banned from the main Overused (OU) tier + Ubers. uu Pokémon banned from Underused (UU) + OU + Ubers. ru Pokémon banned from Rarelyused (RU) + UU + OU + Ubers. nu Pokémon banned from Neverused (NU) + RU + UU + OU + Ubers. pu Pokémon banned from PU + NU + RU + UU + OU + Ubers. lc Pokémon banned from Little Cup format (level 5 Pokémon that are too strong). doubles_ou Pokémon banned in Doubles OU format. monotype Pokémon banned specifically in Monotype format.

docs.title: How to Create a Custom Ban Preset
docs.text: The BattleHUB system is designed to load any .json file placed inside the ban_presets folder. You can create unique rules and formats for your server events in seconds.

docs.title: Step 1: Create the JSON file
docs.text:
Go to the ban_presets/ folder and create a file with the name you want. The filename (without .json) will be the preset ID.

  • Example: no_starters_event.json

docs.title: Step 2: Add the Pokémon List
docs.text:
Add the desired Pokémon as a JSON array. The system is smart and automatically converts names to lowercase, and ignores the cobblemon: prefix if added by mistake:

    [  
      "charizard",  
      "blastoise",  
      "venusaur",  
      "cobblemon:meowscarada"  
    ]

docs.title: Step 3: Use the Preset in a Ladder
docs.text:
Open the configuration of your desired Ladder (in the ladders/ folder) and add your preset ID to the "banPresets" list:

    {  
      "id": "event_singles",  
      "displayName": "Event Battle",  
      "banPresets": [  
        "no_starters_event"  
      ]  
    }

docs.title: Step 4: Reload
docs.text:
To apply and sync the new files, run the reload command from the console or as an in-game administrator:

/bh reload

docs.title: Battle Formats and Presets
docs.text: Cobblemon BattleHUB has native support for multiple competitive and casual battle formats managed by the internal logic of the RankedPreset class. These formats define arena behavior, how many Pokémon enter the field simultaneously, and the structural battle rules.

docs.title: Supported Base Formats
docs.text:
The mod adapts dynamically to the official rules of the Pokémon eSports ecosystem, supporting three main battle structures:

  • Singles (Individual): Traditional 1v1 battles.
  • Doubles: 2v2 battles (the standard format for official VGC).
  • Triples: 3v3 battles with higher strategic complexity.

docs.title: Rule Presets (RankedPreset)
docs.text:
To simplify management and ensure competitive balance in ranked queues, the mod includes Rule Presets pre-configured directly in the code.

Below is the specification for each preset available in the mod:

docs.title: 1. Solo (solo)
docs.text:
• Battle Structure: Singles (1v1)
  • Level Cap: 50
  • Species Clause: Enabled (Prevents duplicate Pokémon on the same team)
  • Item Clause: Enabled (Prevents duplicate held items)
  • Showdown Rules: Standard (Ensures Smogon competitive base validations)

docs.title: 2. Doubles (doubles)
docs.text:
• Battle Structure: Doubles (2v2)
  • Level Cap: 50
  • Species Clause: Enabled
  • Item Clause: Enabled
  • Showdown Rules: Standard

docs.title: 3. Triples (triples)
docs.text:
• Battle Structure: Triples (3v3)
  • Level Cap: 50
  • Species Clause: Enabled
  • Item Clause: Enabled
  • Showdown Rules: Standard

docs.title: 4. Monotype (monotype)
docs.text:
• Battle Structure: Singles (1v1)
  • Level Cap: 50
  • Species Clause: Enabled
  • Item Clause: Enabled
  • Showdown Rules: Standard and Same Type Clause (Requires all Pokémon on the team to share at least one elemental type).

docs.title: 5. Custom (custom)
docs.text:
• Battle Structure: Singles (1v1) by default.
  • Level Cap: 50 by default.
  • Species Clause: Disabled by default.
  • Item Clause: Disabled by default.
  • Description: Allows administrators to create fully custom rules in Ladder files, ignoring the mod's standard competitive restrictions.

docs.title: Gimmicks and Arena Mechanics
docs.text:
Regardless of the selected rule preset, the mod allows you to enable or disable the main battle mechanics introduced across Pokémon generations in Ladder configurations:

  • Mega Evolution (allowMega)
  • Z-Moves (allowZMove)
  • Dynamax / Gigantamax (allowDynamax)
  • Terastal (allowTera)

Performance Optimization

All of the above mechanics are applied when creating the BattleFormat object generated by the mod, ensuring Cobblemon's battle engine validates and applies mechanics in real time without causing desynchronization between client and server.

docs.title: Step-by-Step Setup
docs.text:
1. Ensure Installation: Make sure CarbonChat is installed and functioning correctly on your Fabric environment.
  2. Sync the IDs: Open the CarbonChat channel configuration file and verify your global and local channel IDs. Replace them in globalChannelId and localChannelId inside chatconfig.json.
  3. Customize the Appearance: Modify tabGlobalName and tabLocalName if you want them to display styled names like [GLOBAL] or [ARENA].
  4. Reload on the Server: To apply chat changes in real time without restarting, run the command: /bh reload

docs.title: File Path
docs.text:
config/cobblemon_battlehub/chatconfig.json

Chat Mod Compatibility

Currently, Cobblemon BattleHUB has native and exclusive support for the CarbonChat mod.

If your server uses another chat mod or management system (such as EssentialCmds, LPC, etc.), don’t worry! You can join our Official Discord and open a support ticket. We will happily provide a custom integration for your server's chat system.

docs.title: Default Configuration Template
docs.text:
Below is the default template generated by the ChatConfig class on first startup:

    {  
     "globalChannelId": "global",  
     "localChannelId": "local",  
     "globalCommand": "g",  
     "localCommand": "l",  
     "tabGlobalName": "GLOBAL",  
     "tabLocalName": "LOCAL"  
}

docs.title: 1. Server Identifiers (CarbonChat)
docs.text:
These parameters must match the channel IDs configured inside CarbonChat for BattleHUB communication to work properly.

  • globalChannelId (Default: "global"): The internal ID of the global chat channel used by CarbonChat on the server.
  • localChannelId (Default: "local"): The internal ID of the local chat channel used by CarbonChat (usually for specific arena chats).

docs.title: 2. Client-Side Commands
docs.text:
These are the shortcuts players use in chat to quickly switch between the integrated chat tabs and channels.

  • globalCommand (Default: "g"): The quick command typed in chat to switch to the global channel (e.g. /g <message>).
  • localCommand (Default: "l"): The quick command typed in chat to switch to the local arena channel (e.g. /l <message>).

docs.title: 3. Tab Visual Customization (GUI)
docs.text:
These values change the appearance of the tabs shown to players in the mod's own chat interface (battle HUD).

  • tabGlobalName (Default: "GLOBAL"): The display name players will see on the global channel tab in the mod interface.
  • tabLocalName (Default: "LOCAL"): The display name players will see on the local channel tab in the mod interface.

docs.title: Important
docs.text:
• /fixbattle (Creates a request to your opponent asking to cancel the match due to bugs (neither you nor they lose points))
    • /fixbattle confirm (accepts the opponent's match cancel request)

docs.title: General Commands
docs.text: Command Description Permission /battlehubAliases: /bh, /bhub, /cbh Opens the main Cobblemon BattleHUB interface. battlehub.base /bh status Shows the current server status (e.g. players in queue, season, active matches, etc.). battlehub.status /bh stats Shows your personal stats (e.g. rank, points, matches played, etc.). battlehub.stats.self /bh stats [player] Shows the stats of a specific player. battlehub.stats.other /bh leavequeue Removes you from the matchmaking queue. battlehub.leavequeue /bh leave Removes you from an active match or spectator mode. battlehub.leave /bh spectate Opens the spectator interface to watch active matches. battlehub.spectate /bh quests Opens the interface with your daily and monthly quests. battlehub.quests /bh season Shows information about the current season. battlehub.season.info /bh season stats Shows internal stats and data for the active season. battlehub.season.status /bh season rollover Ends the current season and immediately starts the next one. battlehub.season.rollover /battlehub pokemonstatsAlias: /bh pokemonstats Shows the ranking of the most used Pokémon in ranked matches. battlehub.pokemonstats { .md-typeset__table }

docs.title: Administration and Management Commands
docs.text: Command Description Permission /battlehubAliases: /bh, /bhub, /cbh Opens the main Cobblemon BattleHUB interface. battlehub.base /bh serverstatus Shows general server information and performance data. battlehub.serverstatus /bh reload Instantly reloads all mod configuration files. battlehub.reload /bh clearqueue Clears the global matchmaking queue, removing all players from it. battlehub.clearqueue /bh activation [LICENSE-KEY] Validates and activates the mod license for the server IP. battlehub.activation /bh arenas Shows the status and technical data of loaded arenas (structures). battlehub.arenas /bh battles Displays a list of all real-time duels currently happening. battlehub.battles /bh end [player] Forces immediate termination of the specified player's match. battlehub.end /bh clearban [player] Removes all restrictions and bans applied to the player. battlehub.clearban /bh resetrank [player] Completely resets a player's ranking points and rank. battlehub.resetladder /bh adminstats [player] Shows a complete report of internal information for player moderation. battlehub.adminstats /bh forcestart [player1] [player2] [LadderID] Forces an immediate match start between two players in a specific category. battlehub.forcestart /bh leaderboard [LadderID] [Limit] Displays the leaderboard for the specified category (Ladder) in chat. battlehub.leaderboard { .md-typeset__table }

docs.title: Owner/Director Commands
docs.text:
Command Description Permission /battlehubAliases: /bh, /bhub, /cbh Opens the main Cobblemon BattleHUB interface. battlehub.base /bh resetlimitshop [player] Refreshes and resets all shop purchase limits for the specified player. battlehub.resetlimitshop /bh rollpokemonshop Resets and forces a new rotation of Pokémon available for purchase in the shop. battlehub.rollpokemonshop /bh structures list Displays a list of all currently generated and active arenas (structures). battlehub.structures.list /bh structures clear Removes and destroys all currently spawned arenas (structures). battlehub.structures.clear /bh structures tp [ArenaID] Instantly teleports you to the coordinates of the arena indicated by the ID. battlehub.structures.tp /bh structures build [all/ArenaID] Builds a specific arena (by ID) or forces generation of all arenas at once. battlehub.structures.build { .md-typeset__table }

🏆 Tournament Commands → Click to go to the page.

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text:
• Required Dependencies:

    • Impactor 5.3.5 (Currently this is the only economy supported by the mod!)
    • Cobblemon
    • LuckPerms 5.4.140
    • CarbonChat 3.0.0-beta.32

  • Optional Dependencies:

Development Notes

  • We prioritize compatibility with your ecosystem. If your server uses a specific economy or chat mod, please report this need via a ticket on our Discord. We will evaluate and prioritize the requested integration."

docs.title: Frequently Asked Questions
docs.text: Welcome to the FAQ section of Cobblemon BattleHUB. Below, we have compiled answers to the most common questions from our community of server administrators and owners.

docs.title: 1. Where should I host images for the Shop?
docs.text:
To ensure 2D image loading in the mod interface is fast and free of security blocks (CORS), we strongly recommend using Imgur.

  • How to do it: Upload your image to Imgur, right-click it, and choose "Copy Image Address" (the link must end with .png or .jpg).
  • Paste that link directly into the imageUrls field in your shop.json or in the webhook.json settings.
  • Technical Note: The mod uses its own system that downloads the image only once on the player's machine and caches it in memory, ensuring high performance even with dozens of items in the shop!

docs.title: 2. How can I add custom arenas using my server's builds?
docs.text:
Cobblemon BattleHUB instantiates physical arenas in the battle world using Minecraft's native Structures (.nbt) system. To customize arena appearances, you do not need to change the mod—just use a Datapack!

  1. On our Discord, we provide the download for the mod's base Structures pack.
  2. Save your custom arena builds as (.nbt) files using Structure Blocks in-game.
  3. Replace the original .nbt files in the pack with your own, keeping the same filenames.
  4. Place the datapack folder inside world/datapacks/ on your server and run /reload.

The next time a battle starts, the mod will build your custom arena!

docs.title: 3. How do I purchase the mod and the license for my server?
docs.text:
The acquisition process is secure, fast, and 100% automated! To get your copy of Cobblemon BattleHUB and your activation key (License Key), follow the steps below:

  1. Join our community on Discord.
  2. Navigate to the Shop channel/section.
  3. Select the Cobblemon BattleHUB package and add it to your cart.
  4. You will be redirected to complete the payment securely on the official Stripe website.
  5. Once the payment is approved, the system will instantly send you the mod .jar file and your exclusive License Key.
  6. Just place the mod in the server, run /bh activation [YOUR_KEY], and enjoy!

Still have questions?

If your technical or configuration question was not answered here or on the previous documentation pages, don’t hesitate to open a Support Ticket on our Discord. Our team will be ready to help you set up the perfect eSports experience for your server!

docs.title: Tournament System Workflow
docs.text: The Cobblemon BattleHUB tournament system is fully automated and integrated with the server's battle ecosystem. It manages the entire competition lifecycle — from registration opening to crowning the champion — without administrators needing to manually handle external brackets.

docs.title: The Tournament Lifecycle
docs.text:
A tournament execution flow passes through 6 well-defined logical states, controlled by the manager's Status property:

[UPCOMING] ➔ [REGISTRATION] ➔ [SEEDING] ➔ [IN_PROGRESS] ➔ [FINISHED]

docs.title: 1. Initialization and Profiles (UPCOMING)
docs.text: The mod loads all tournament definitions and structural rules directly from the profiles folder. The tournament stays on standby, displaying reward details, scheduled date, and format rules in the player's UI.

docs.title: 2. Registration Opening (REGISTRATION)
docs.text:
When the manualRegistrationOpen parameter is set to true (or activated via command), players gain access to register for the tournament through the in-game interface.

  • The mod strictly limits registrations to the maximum defined by maxParticipants.
  • Each participant's registration data is saved by associating their UUID with their nickname in the database.

docs.title: 3. Bracket Drawing and Pairing (SEEDING)
docs.text: When the command /bh tournament <ID> roll is executed, the system closes registrations and starts building the single elimination bracket.

docs.title: 4. Convocação de Partidas (IN_PROGRESS)
docs.text:
Assim que o chaveamento é gerado, o torneio entra em andamento. O sistema de ticks monitora ativamente as partidas e gerencia os combates através de "Blocos de Luta":

  1. Chamada de Partida: O administrador ou o sistema envia a ordem de início para um bloco. Os dois competidores recebem um alerta global e um temporizador de preparação é iniciado.
  2. Boss Bar de Transmissão: Uma Boss Bar global é exibida no topo da tela para todos os jogadores online do servidor que não estão lutando, indicando o progresso atual do torneio (ex: "Torneio Mensal - Fase 1").

docs.title: 5. Execução Automática de Batalhas
docs.text:
• The called players must prepare their team and click "Ready" in the mod interface.
  • Once both are ready (or if the preparation time is forced), the mod instantly starts the Cobblemon match using the tournament profile's configured Ladder (e.g. tourney_solo).

docs.title: 6. Dynamic Advancement and Completion (FINISHED)
docs.text:
• Once the battle session ends, the mod intercepts the in-game result.
  • The winner is instantly advanced in the bracket to create the next phase block (e.g. _phase_2_1 or _final_1).
  • The loser is eliminated from the competition.
  • The process repeats until only one active competitor remains. When the final winner is declared, the status changes to FINISHED and the champion's name is permanently recorded in the history (lastChampion).

docs.title: Anti-W.O. Mechanism for Preparation Time
docs.text:
To prevent absent players (AFK) from blocking the progress of the entire tournament, the mod strictly monitors the preparation timeout:

  • Each called match receives a deadline in milliseconds.
  • If the timer expires and one of the competitors has not confirmed readiness, the system executes automatic elimination:
  • If Player 2 marked ready but Player 1 did not, Player 2 is declared the winner by W.O.
  • If neither player marked ready, the system eliminates both or advances the one with the better seed/readiness.
  • The disqualification announcement is sent to chat and the bracket is immediately updated in the database.

docs.title: Server Requirements
docs.text:
Before starting the installation process, make sure your infrastructure meets all the required dependencies listed below:

  • Minecraft: 1.21.1
  • Fabric Loader: 0.17.3 or higher
  • Fabric API: 0.116.6+1.21.1 or higher
  • Java: 21 or higher
  • Cobblemon: 1.7.3
  • Fabric Language Kotlin: 1.13.0+kotlin.2.1.0 or higher

docs.title: Installation:
docs.text: The installation process for Cobblemon BattleHUB is simple and straightforward. Follow the steps below to prepare your server correctly.

docs.title: Step 1: Initial Installation
docs.text:
1. Transfer the mod .jar file to your server's mods directory.
  2. Start (or restart) the server to load the mod into memory.
  3. The mod will automatically generate the cobblemon_battlehub folder inside your server's config/ directory during startup.

docs.title: Step 2: Configure the Files
docs.text: Navigate to the newly created config/cobblemon_battlehub/ folder and perform the required basic adjustments in the following files so the mod knows where to operate:

docs.title: fallbackarenadisconnect.json
docs.text:
This file defines where the player will be teleported when disconnecting or leaving the arena. Set your server lobby coordinates:

json
{
  "dimension": "multiworld:void",
  "x": 777.5,
  "y": 77.0,
  "z": 777.5,
  "yaw": 0.0,
  "pitch": 0.0
}

docs.title: arenas.json
docs.text:
Here you should configure the dimension where the arena structures will be generated:

{
  "dimension": "multiworld:battles",
  "arenas": [
    // Configure your arenas here
 ]
}

docs.title: Step 3: Mod Activation
docs.text:
To unlock the mod, you need to activate your license inside the server:

  • 1. Join your server.
  • 2. Use the activation command: /bh activation [LICENSE-KEY]
    • (The [KEY] is sent to you immediately after payment confirmation via Stripe).

Attention: Single Server License

This license is valid exclusively for a single active instance. The security system will permanently bind your activation key to the first IP that validates it. Multiple activations or key sharing are not possible.

Don't have an activation key yet?

Your license key (License Key) is automatically generated and sent to your email as soon as your Stripe payment is confirmed. To acquire yours, join our Official Discord and open a support ticket.

docs.title: Step 4: Finalization
docs.text: After configuring the JSON files and activating your key, run the following command on the server to apply the changes: /bh reload

docs.title: Done! The mod is configured, activated, and ready to be used on your server.
Welcome to Cobblemon BattleHUB
docs.text: Cobblemon BattleHUB is the ultimate infrastructure for servers looking to elevate competitive play and organize Pokémon battles within Minecraft. Built to run natively on the Fabric platform, the mod integrates a complete ecosystem of matchmaking, automated tournament management, and a robust economy, turning your server into a true Pokémon eSports arena.

docs.title: Main Features
docs.text:
Cobblemon BattleHUB goes beyond a simple duel system, providing a full management framework for administrators and an immersive experience for players.

  • Advanced Duel System: Intelligent matchmaking with support for arenas instantiated in separate dimensions, avoiding overload or interference with the server's main world.
  • Automated Tournament Management: Automatic bracket organization and registration control, simplifying competitive event execution.
  • Integrated Economy and Shop: Dedicated in-game shop to reward player activity and sell strategic battle items.
  • Statistics and History: Detailed performance tracking so players can follow their battle history and ranking evolution.
  • License Security: Exclusive protection with IP-based license authentication directly on the backend, ensuring mod integrity and exclusivity.
  • Native Network Integration: Fully compatible with the Fabric ecosystem, offering formatted chat support (via Carbon) and performance telemetry.

docs.title: Development and Authorship
docs.text: This project was fully conceived, designed, and programmed by F4xizzz. The entire mod development (in Java/Fabric), backend architecture in Node.js with MongoDB, and license security and validation systems were independently built to ensure high performance, optimization, and stability.

docs.title: Support and Community
docs.text:
If you want to ask configuration questions, report bugs, suggest new mechanics, or follow upcoming updates closely, join our official channel.

Official Community

Contact us directly through our official server on Discord. This documentation is constantly updated to ensure the best configuration and usage experience for Cobblemon BattleHUB on your server.

docs.title: Ladder Configuration
docs.text: Ladders (Battle Categories) define the competitive and casual matchmaking formats in Cobblemon BattleHUB. They control everything: team size, ban rules, level adjustments, and which battle mechanics (gimmicks) are allowed in the arena.

docs.title: Directory Path
docs.text: config/cobblemon_battlehub/ladders/

docs.title: Default Configuration Template
docs.text:
Below is the default JSON template to create or edit a Ladder. It is based directly on the fields in the Ladder.java class:

    {  
      "id": "singles_ranked",  
      "queueLabel": "Ranked Queue",  
      "displayName": "Ranked Singles Lv. 50",  
      "description": "Official Ranked Singles format.",  
      "ranked": true,  
      "battleTypeId": "singles",  
      "requiredTeamSize": 6,  
      "adjustLevel": 50,  
      "enforceSpeciesClause": true,  
      "enforceItemClause": true,  
      "banPresets": [  
        "ou"  
      ],  
      "bannedSpeciesKeys": [],  
      "bannedItemKeys": [],  
      "bannedAbilityKeys": [],  
      "bannedMoveKeys": [],  
      "bannedTierKeys": [],  
      "showdownRules": [],  
      "allowRestrictedPokemon": false,  
      "allowMythical": false,  
      "allowParadox": false,  
      "allowMega": true,  
      "allowZMove": true,  
      "allowDynamax": false,  
      "allowTera": true  
    }

docs.title: 1. Identification and Display (Matchmaking UI)
docs.text:
• id: The category's unique internal ID (must match the filename exactly without the .json extension).
  • queueLabel: The queue name shown in network packets and interfaces (e.g. Ranked Queue, Quick Queue, Tournament).
  • displayName: Friendly title displayed to players inside the mod menus.
  • description: Brief description explaining the queue rules or purpose in the interface.
  • ranked (true/false): Determines whether the category awards ranking points (Elo/Glicko-2) and updates the global leaderboard.

docs.title: 2. Combat Structural Rules
docs.text:
• battleTypeId: The battle field format. Supported values:
  • "singles" (1v1)
  • "doubles" (2v2)
  • "triples" (3v3)
  • requiredTeamSize: Minimum number of Pokémon the player must have in their party to enter the matchmaking queue (Default: 6).
  • adjustLevel: Level to which all team Pokémon will be temporarily adjusted during battle. Use 0 to disable level adjustment and fight at the Pokémon's actual level.

docs.title: 3. Competitive Clauses
docs.text:
• enforceSpeciesClause (true/false): Prevents the player from using two or more Pokémon of the same species on the same team.
  • enforceItemClause (true/false): Prevents two or more Pokémon from holding the same equipped item.

docs.title: 4. Meta Filters (Restricted, Mythical, and Paradox Pokémon)
docs.text:
• allowRestrictedPokemon (true/false): If disabled, bans restricted-tier legendary Pokémon (according to official VGC rules).
  • allowMythical (true/false): Enables or disables the use of Mythical Pokémon (e.g. Mew, Celebi, Jirachi).
  • allowParadox (true/false): Enables or disables the use of Paradox Pokémon (e.g. Great Tusk, Iron Valiant).

docs.title: 5. Arena Mechanics (Gimmicks)
docs.text:
• allowMega (true/false): Allows or bans Mega Evolution in battle.
  • allowZMove (true/false): Allows or bans the use of Z-Moves.
  • allowDynamax (true/false): Allows or bans Dynamax and Gigantamax mechanics.
  • allowTera (true/false): Allows or bans Terastal (Paldea phenomenon).

docs.title: 6. Ban Lists & Custom Rules (Showdown)
docs.text:
• banPresets: A list containing the IDs of ban preset JSON files you want to inherit (e.g. ["ou"], ["lc"], ["legendaries"]).
  • bannedSpeciesKeys: Manual list to ban specific Pokémon by ID (e.g. ["charizard", "mewtwo"]).
  • bannedItemKeys: Manual list to ban specific items.
  • bannedAbilityKeys: Manual list to ban specific abilities.
  • bannedMoveKeys: Manual list to ban specific moves.
  • showdownRules: Allows injecting additional rules interpreted directly by Showdown into Cobblemon's engine (e.g. ["Same Type Clause"] for Monotype).

docs.title: Dynamic Suffix Logic (Custom Duels)
docs.text:
The Ladder.java class includes an intelligent Dynamic Suffix system for when two players challenge each other to a direct duel and decide to disable certain mechanics on the fly (using the mod's invite UI).

If players choose to disable gimmicks in the menu, the mod generates an in-memory modified copy of the Ladder by adding the following invisible suffixes to the ID:

  • _nomega (Disables Mega Evolution)
  • _noz (Disables Z-Moves)
  • _nodyna (Disables Dynamax)
  • _notera (Disables Terastal)

Example: If your base Ladder is singles_ranked and the player disables Tera on the invite screen, the mod temporarily creates a copy under the ID singles_ranked_notera with the option "allowTera": false applied automatically, ensuring the battle proceeds fairly and without rule desynchronization.

docs.title: Practical Guide: Creating an Event Ladder (Little Cup)
docs.text:
Cobblemon BattleHUB lets you create fully custom battle formats for temporary events, special tournaments, or new permanent queues.

For this guide, we will create a category based on Smogon’s classic Little Cup (LC) format: only baby Pokémon or their first evolution stage, adjusted to Level 5, with all power mechanics (Mega, Z-Move, Dynamax, and Tera) completely disabled.

docs.title: Custom Directory Path
docs.text:
All custom categories should be created inside the customization subfolder to avoid mixing them with the mod’s default queues:

config/cobblemon_battlehub/ladders/custom_ladders/

docs.title: Step 1: Creating the Ladder JSON File
docs.text:
Go to the custom_ladders/ folder, create a file named little_cup_event.json, and add the following structure:

    {  
      "id": "little_cup_event",  
      "queueLabel": "Event Queue",  
      "displayName": "Little Cup Event",  
      "description": "Only Level 5 Pokémon! No Megas, Z-Moves, Dynamax, or Terastal.",  
      "ranked": false,  
      "battleTypeId": "singles",  
      "requiredTeamSize": 6,  
      "adjustLevel": 5,  
      "enforceSpeciesClause": true,  
      "enforceItemClause": true,  
      "banPresets": [  
        "lc"  
      ],  
      "bannedSpeciesKeys": [],  
      "bannedItemKeys": [],  
      "bannedAbilityKeys": [],  
      "bannedMoveKeys": [],  
      "bannedTierKeys": [],  
      "showdownRules": [],  
      "allowRestrictedPokemon": false,  
      "allowMythical": false,  
      "allowParadox": false,  
      "allowMega": false,  
      "allowZMove": false,  
      "allowDynamax": false,  
      "allowTera": false  
    }

docs.title: What are we configuring here?
docs.text:
1. id: Set to little_cup_event (must exactly match the JSON file name).
  2. adjustLevel: Set to 5. All Pokémon will be temporarily lowered or raised to level 5 for the battle.
  3. banPresets: We load the lc preset that ships with the mod. This will automatically ban strong stage 1 Pokémon for the format (like Scyther, Gligar, etc.).
  4. Disabled Mechanics: We set all gimmick properties (allowMega, allowTera, etc.) to false to ensure a pure early-gen strategic battle.

docs.title: Step 2: Registering the Queue on the Server
docs.text:
Creating the JSON file in the custom folder makes the mod load it into memory, but it will not yet appear in the matchmaking menu. To make the queue available to players, you must enable it in the main server config file.

  1. Open the main config file at: config/cobblemon_battlehub/server_config.json
  2. Add your new Ladder ID (little_cup_event) to the appropriate list. Since we set "ranked": false in our JSON, add it to the casual list:
      "activeCasualLadders": [  
    "singles_50_casual",  
    "doubles_50_casual",  
    "little_cup_event"  
  ],

Organization Tip

If you want this queue to award points and ranking on the server, simply change "ranked": true in your JSON file and register it under "activeRankedLadders" in server_config.json.

docs.title: Step 3: Syncing the Changes
docs.text:
After saving the files, you do not need to restart the physical server. Open the server console or run the command in-game as an administrator:

/bh reload

Done! The Little Cup Event queue will appear instantly in players’ menus with the rules, level adjustments, and Pokémon restrictions you just defined.

docs.title: Pokémon Rotation Configuration
docs.text: The Pokémon rotation system in Cobblemon BattleHUB works like a dynamic showcase or a roulette. At configurable intervals, the mod selects a list of Pokémon with randomized stats (IVs) and Shiny odds to display in the shop with prices calculated in real time.

docs.title: File Path
docs.text: config/cobblemon_battlehub/pkmrotation_config.json

docs.title: Default Configuration Template
docs.text:
Below is the default JSON template generated automatically on the first mod startup:

    {  
      "intervalHours": 12,  
      "pokemonsPerRotation": 12,  
      "shinyChancePercent": 2.5,  
      "shinyPriceMultiplier": 3.0,  
      "currency": "Dollars",  
      "currencyName": "<green><bold>%price%$</bold></green>",  
      "pools": [  
        {  
          "name": "Common",  
          "weight": 80,  
          "basePrice": 500.0,  
          "pokemons": ["absol", "bidoof", "gastly", "geodude", "psyduck"]  
        },  
        {  
          "name": "Legendary",  
          "weight": 3,  
          "basePrice": 15000.0,  
          "pokemons": ["articuno", "mewtwo", "rayquaza", "lugia"]  
        }  
      ],  
      "ivTiers": [  
        {  
          "perfectIvs": 1,  
          "weight": 50,  
          "priceMultiplier": 1.0  
        },  
        {  
          "perfectIvs": 6,  
          "weight": 1,  
          "priceMultiplier": 6.0  
        }  
      ]  
    }

docs.title: 1. Global Rotation Parameters
docs.text:
• intervalHours (Default: 12): The time interval (in hours) for the rotation to automatically refresh and generate a new list of Pokémon for sale.
  • pokemonsPerRotation (Default: 12): The number of Pokémon cards displayed simultaneously in the shop rotation tab.
  • shinyChancePercent (Default: 2.5): The chance (percentage, from 0.0 to 100.0) for each selected Pokémon to be Shiny.
  • shinyPriceMultiplier (Default: 3.0): The multiplier applied to the final price if the selected Pokémon is Shiny.
  • currency: The internal currency ID used by ImpactorBridge for purchases (e.g. "Dollars" or "BattlePoints").
  • currencyName: The visual price format in the menu. Supports MiniMessage colors and the %price% variable.

docs.title: Prevention of Abandonment and Safe Rescue
docs.text: To prevent competitive abuse (such as griefing, Alt+F4 to avoid losing points, or spying) and to ensure players never become trapped in arena dimensions due to connection issues or crashes, Cobblemon BattleHUB implements a robust security infrastructure and the Leaver Buster punishment system.

docs.title: 1. Disconnect Manager
docs.text: The system constantly monitors the life cycle of each player’s connection from the moment the arena is instantiated.

docs.title: State Save Flow
docs.text:
Once a match is paired (exactly when the BattleManager creates a Session), the mod performs the following safety steps:

  1. Location Snapshot: The mod records the player’s exact location in the overworld (X, Y, Z), the dimension, camera rotation (Yaw/Pitch), and original game mode.
  2. Offline/Fallback Registration: If the player suffers an abrupt disconnection (Alt+F4, power loss, or client crash) during combat, the server prevents them from logging back into the instantiated arena.
  3. Emergency Rescue: When attempting to reconnect, the mod intercepts the player’s login and restores their original game mode and inventory, then safely teleports them to the global coordinates configured in fallbackarenadisconnect.json (usually the server spawn or lobby).

docs.title: 2. Leaver Buster System (Desertion Punishment)
docs.text: To keep the competitive ecosystem healthy, abandoning ranked matches (whether by timed-out turns or by intentionally leaving the server) is severely punished.

docs.title: What Counts as Abandonment?
docs.text:
A forced surrender loss (W.O) is declared in the following scenarios:

  • Disconnection: The player closes the game or drops connection and their entity is removed from the server.
  • Prolonged Inactivity (AFK): The player exceeds the consecutive turn limit where autopilot is activated (as configured by maxAfkAutopilotRounds in server_config.json).

docs.title: Consequences for the Leaver
docs.text:
1. The active opponent receives an automatic W.O. victory with a congratulatory chat message.
  2. The leaver receives an automatic loss in their stats profile and loses Rating () as usual.
  3. The player is applied a temporary ranked ban. The duration of this ban is cumulative and prevents the user from re-entering the competitive queue for a set period.

Queue Ban Warning: When attempting to enter matchmaking with an active ban, the player receives a formatted remaining time warning (e.g. "You are banned from the competitive queue for another 01h 15m").

docs.title: 3. Safe Cancellation
docs.text: In rare cases of extreme desynchronization where the match becomes stuck due to an unexpected battle engine response or an internal tick error, players can use the safety command /fixbattle.

docs.title: How Does Safe Cancellation Memory Work?
docs.text:
To prevent a player from abusing the command to escape an imminent loss, the system requires a Mutual Agreement:

  1. When a player types the command, they send a cancellation request.
  2. The opponent receives a visual alert that a technical safety cancellation has been requested.
  3. If the opponent agrees and also types the command within an acceptable timeframe, the mod cleanly ends the arena:
  4. Neither player loses or gains Rating ().
  5. Both players and their teams are safely teleported back to their original locations by safeRescue.
  6. The instantiated arena is released and cleared from the server immediately.

docs.title: Products and Bundles
docs.text: The Cobblemon BattleHUB shop is divided into two parts: the static storefront (fixed products and bundles) and the rotation roulette. The shop.json file manages the static section, where you can sell items, Pokémon bundles, cosmetics (via commands), or perks (VIPs).

docs.title: File Path
docs.text: config/cobblemon_battlehub/shop.json

docs.title: JSON Structure
docs.text:
Below is an example of the structure generated automatically by the mod, containing a "Bundle" (a package with multiple commands/Pokémon) and a regular "Item":

    {  
      "shop": [  
        {  
          "id": "example_bundle",  
          "name": "Tinkaton Bundle",  
          "type": "bundle",  
          "description": "Purchase this amazing pink bundle with the full evolution line!",  
          "price": 1500.0,  
          "discount": 15,  
          "currency": "Dollars",  
          "currencyName": "<green><bold>%price%$</bold></green>",  
          "featured": true,  
          "scale": 1.3,  
          "offsetX": 0,  
          "offsetY": 0,  
          "width": 120,  
          "height": 160,  
          "maxPurchases": 1,  
          "imageUrls": [  
            "https://i.imgur.com/ZGNJEw7.png"  
          ],  
          "includedItems": [  
            "Shiny Tinkaton with Perfect IVs",  
            "5x Diamonds"  
          ],  
          "commands": [  
            "pokegiveother %player% tinkaton s min_perfect_ivs=6",  
            "give %player% diamond 5"  
          ]  
        },  
        {  
          "id": "abilitypatch",  
          "name": "AbilityPatch",  
          "type": "item",  
          "description": "Changes your Pokémon's ability to its Hidden Ability.",  
          "price": 1000.0,  
          "discount": 0,  
          "currency": "Dollars",  
          "currencyName": "<green><bold>%price%$</bold></green>",  
          "featured": false,  
          "scale": 1.0,  
          "offsetX": 0,  
          "offsetY": 0,  
          "width": 60,  
          "height": 60,  
          "maxPurchases": 0,  
          "imageUrls": [  
            "https://i.imgur.com/T9VPbyx.png"  
          ],  
          "includedItems": [  
            "1x Ability Patch"  
          ],  
          "commands": [  
            "give %player% cobblemon:ability_patch"  
          ]  
        }  
      ]  
    }

docs.title: 1. Identification and Metadata
docs.text:
• id: Unique string that identifies the product in the database.

Warning:

Never change a product’s ID after players have purchased it, or they will lose purchase history and may bypass purchase limits.

  • name: The product name (supports MiniMessage and legacy colors).
  • type: Defines how the product is displayed. Common values are "item" or "bundle".
  • description: The description text shown in the item tooltip.
  • featured: (true/false) If true, the product appears highlighted in the top shelf of the store at a larger size.

docs.title: 2. Economy and Pricing
docs.text:
• price: The product base price.
  • discount: Discount percentage value (e.g. 15 = 15% off). The mod displays a promotional badge visually.
  • currency: The internal currency ID that ImpactorBridge will charge the player.
  • currencyName: The visual formatting of how currency and price appear in the menu. Use %price% where the number should be inserted.

docs.title: 3. Visual Rendering (GUI)
docs.text:
• imageUrls: List of direct image links (e.g. Imgur). The mod downloads, caches them via WebImageCache, and renders them in the menu. If there is more than one, they act like an animated GIF.
  • width / height: Base resolution at which the image is drawn in the menu.
  • scale: Icon size multiplier in the menu (default: 1.0).
  • offsetX / offsetY: Fine pixel adjustments to move the image if it appears off-center on the card.

docs.title: 4. Limits and Rewards
docs.text:
• maxPurchases: Maximum number of times a player can buy this item in their lifetime. If set to 0, the item can be purchased infinitely.
  • includedItems: A list of texts shown in the floating menu tooltip describing what is included in the bundle.
  • commands: The list of console commands executed when the purchase is approved. The %player% variable is replaced with the buyer’s username.

docs.title: Quest System Configuration (quests.json)
docs.text: The quest system in Cobblemon BattleHUB rewards player activity and loyalty through daily, weekly, and permanent challenges. The quests.json file is the core where all quests, completion goals, and reward commands are stored and edited.

docs.title: File Path
docs.text: config/cobblemon\_battlehub/quests.json

docs.title: How the System Works
docs.text:
The system behavior is orchestrated dynamically by the QuestManager.java class on every login and end of match:

  1. Automatic Generation: On first startup, if quests.json is not found, the mod creates the file automatically and populates it with more than 90 pre-defined quests.
  2. Intelligent Random Selection: For daily and weekly quests, the mod does not give the player the entire catalog. It randomly selects a subset of IDs based on a seed generated from the current date combined with the player’s UUID. This ensures each player receives different quests each cycle!
  3. Safe Persistence: The player’s current progress (how many steps they completed, what has already been redeemed, and reset times) is saved asynchronously to the database.

docs.title: Configuration Structure Template
docs.text:
Below is the simplified structure of quests.json that demonstrates how quests and global parameters are defined:

    {  
      "daily_quests_per_player": 2,  
      "weekly_quests_per_player": 5,  
      "daily_quests": [  
        {  
          "id": "daily_01",  
          "type": "PLAY_MATCHES",  
          "title": "First Step",  
          "description": "Play 1 match in any queue.",  
          "targetAmount": 1,  
          "typeFilter": null,  
          "rewardDescription": "50 Dollars",  
          "rewardCommands": [  
            "eco deposit 50 dollar %player%"  
          ]  
        }  
      ],  
      "weekly_quests": [  
        {  
          "id": "weekly_01",  
          "type": "WIN_RANKED",  
          "title": "Arena Veteran",  
          "description": "Win 15 Ranked matches.",  
          "targetAmount": 15,  
          "typeFilter": null,  
          "rewardDescription": "1000 Battle Points",  
          "rewardCommands": [  
            "eco deposit 1000 battlepoint %player%"  
          ]  
        }  
      ],  
      "permanent_quests": [  
        {  
          "id": "perm_mono_fire",  
          "type": "MASTER_MONOTYPE",  
          "title": "Master of Flames",  
          "description": "Reach 100 wins using Fire Monotype.",  
          "targetAmount": 100,  
          "typeFilter": "fire",  
          "rewardDescription": "Fire Master Tag",  
          "rewardCommands": [  
            "lp user %player% parent add master_fire"  
          ]  
        }  
      ]  
    }

docs.title: 1. Global Selection Parameters
docs.text:
• daily_quests_per_player (Default: 2): Determines how many daily quests are drawn from the global pool and assigned to each player every 24 hours.
  • weekly_quests_per_player (Default: 5): Determines how many weekly quests are drawn from the pool and assigned to each player every 7 days.

docs.title: 2. Quest List Parameters (daily_quests, weekly_quests, permanent_quests)
docs.text:
Each quest object in the lists has the following required properties:

  • id: The quest’s technical identifier. It is used by the database to save whether the player has completed or redeemed the reward. Never duplicate IDs!
  • type: The mission action trigger that connects to the mod’s statistics. It must match the names listed in QuestType.java exactly (e.g. PLAY_MATCHES, WIN_RANKED, USE_TYPE, MASTER_MONOTYPE).
  • title: The visual, eye-catching title displayed to the player in the quest menu and chat notifications.
  • description: The text explanation that tells the player exactly what they need to do to complete the quest.
  • targetAmount: The number of times the trigger must be activated for the quest to be considered complete (e.g. Win 15 matches, KO 50 Pokémon).
  • typeFilter: A condition filter. It can limit the quest to a specific format (such as singles or doubles) or to a Pokémon type in the team (such as fire, electric, water). Set it to null if the quest is generic.
  • rewardDescription: The visual reward description shown to the player when they complete or redeem the quest.
  • rewardCommands: A list of commands that the server console executes immediately when the player redeems the reward. Supports the %player% or {player} variable to automatically inject the name of the player who completed the objective.

docs.title: How to Reload New Quests
docs.text:
If you add new quests, change descriptions, or modify rewards directly in quests.json, you can apply the changes in real time and sync the interface for all players using the command:

/bh reload

docs.title: Practical Guide: Creating Custom Quests
docs.text:
The Cobblemon BattleHUB system lets you create an unlimited number of challenges for your players. You can encourage the use of underused types, reward aggressive playstyles, or create lasting prestige milestones.

Below, we will walk through how to structure and register three practical examples of custom quests directly in your quests.json file.

docs.title: Example 1: Daily Type Quest
docs.text:
In this example, we will create a simple daily quest that encourages the player to try a Grass-type Pokémon in their team.

  • Objective: Participate in 1 battle using at least one Grass-type Pokémon in the team.
  • Reward: $100 in server currency.

docs.title: JSON Structure:
docs.text:
Add this block inside the "daily_quests" list in your quests.json file:

    {  
      "id": "daily_custom_grass",  
      "type": "USE_TYPE",  
      "title": "Strong Roots",  
      "description": "Complete 1 battle using at least one Grass-type Pokémon in your main team.",  
      "targetAmount": 1,  
      "typeFilter": "grass",  
      "rewardDescription": "100 Coins",  
      "rewardCommands": [  
        "eco deposit 100 dollar %player%"  
      ]  
    }

docs.title: Example 2: Weekly Fast Win Quest
docs.text:
In this weekly challenge, we reward players who finish matches extremely quickly and aggressively in ranked queues.

  • Objective: Win 3 ranked matches in 10 turns or less.
  • Reward: 500 Battle Points (BP).

docs.title: JSON Structure:
docs.text:
Add this block inside the "weekly_quests" list in your quests.json file:

    {  
      "id": "weekly_custom_fast_win",  
      "type": "WIN_FAST",  
      "title": "Lightning Strike",  
      "description": "Win 3 arena matches with a maximum of 10 turns per match.",  
      "targetAmount": 3,  
      "typeFilter": null,  
      "rewardDescription": "500 Battle Points",  
      "rewardCommands":[  
        "eco deposit 500 battlepoint %player%"  
      ]  
    }

docs.title: Example 3: Permanent Mastery Quest (Dragon Master)
docs.text:
Permanent quests act like lifetime achievements. They do not reset on the daily or weekly cycle. In this example, we will create a milestone for players who reach 150 wins using a Dragon Monotype team.

  • Objective: Reach 150 wins with a Dragon Monotype team.
  • Reward: Grant an exclusive Tag via LuckPerms and announce it globally.

docs.title: JSON Structure:
docs.text:
Add this block inside the "permanent_quests" list in your quests.json file:

{  
  "id": "perm_custom_dragon_master",  
  "type": "MASTER_MONOTYPE",  
  "title": "Dragon Sovereign",  
  "description": "Reach the historic milestone of 150 competitive wins using a Dragon Monotype team.",  
  "targetAmount": 150,  
  "typeFilter": "dragon",  
  "rewardDescription": "Legendary Tag [Dragon Master]",  
  "rewardCommands": [  
    "lp user %player% parent add mestre_dragao",  
    "broadcast &6[BattleHUB] &e%player% has achieved ultimate mastery and is now a Dragon Master!"  
  ]  
}

docs.title: Best Practices When Creating New Quests
docs.text:
To ensure system stability and avoid compatibility issues with saved database data, make sure to follow these three simple rules:

  1. Unique and Standardized IDs: Never use the same ID for two different quests. We recommend using a clear prefix for each list, such as daily_..., weekly_..., or perm_....
  2. Filter Normalization: The typeFilter parameter reads directly from Cobblemon elemental types and format IDs. Always enter values in lowercase (e.g. fire instead of Fire, doubles instead of Doubles).
  3. Command Syntax: You can chain as many console commands as you'd like in the "rewardCommands" list. The system will automatically replace the %player% and {player} variables with the player's current nickname.

docs.title: How to Apply the New Quests
docs.text:
After saving changes to quests.json, apply the updates instantly to active players on the server by running the reload command:

/bh reload

docs.title: Quest Types and Progress Triggers
docs.text:
Cobblemon BattleHUB ships with a wide range of battle objectives. Each quest's behavior is determined by its type.

Below is the detailed table of all natively supported quest types, how progress is calculated, and how to use the typeFilter parameter in each scenario.

docs.title: Quest Trigger and Behavior Table (QuestType)
docs.text: Technical Key (type) Filter (typeFilter) Behavior and Progress PLAY_MATCHES Not used Increases progress for every completed match (Ranked or Casual). WIN_MATCHES Not used Increases progress whenever the player wins a match in any queue. WIN_RANKED Format (e.g. singles, doubles) Increases progress if the player wins a Competitive match that matches the filter format. If the filter is null, any ranked format is accepted. PLAY_RANKED Format (e.g. singles, doubles) Increases progress if the player completes a Competitive match in the specified format. PLAY_CASUAL Not used Increases progress when completing matches in the server’s Casual queues. WIN_STREAK Not used Tracks consecutive wins. If the player wins, they gain progress. Warning: If the player loses a match, this quest’s progress resets to ! USE_TYPE Elemental type (e.g. fire, ghost) Increases progress if the player completes a battle using at least one Pokémon of the specified type in their main party. WIN_WITH_ALIVE Not used Increases progress whenever the player wins a match (focused on survival strategies). WIN_FAST Not used Increases progress if the player wins the match in a total of turns or less (targetAmount defines the value). PLAY_LONG Not used Increases progress if the match lasts turns or more (or exceeds the overall 20-turn limit). WIN_BY_FORFEIT Not used Increases progress if the opponent forfeits the match (surrender/Alt+F4), awarding victory to the player. PLAY_NO_FORFEIT Not used Increases progress by completing a full match without either side forfeiting. PLAY_MONOTYPE Not used Increases progress if the player enters a battle with a team that follows Monotype rules (all Pokémon share a common type). KNOCKOUT_TOTAL Not used Balances knockout progress. Grants points if the player wins the match, or point if they lose, reflecting average battle aggression. MASTER_MONOTYPE Elemental type (e.g. water, steel) Direct Profile Sync: This type tracks total Monotype wins in the database. The mod automatically syncs the player’s progress with the total Monotype victories recorded in their global stats profile (StatsManager).

docs.title: How the Format Filter Works
docs.text: The typeFilter parameter uses a partial validation system (formatMatches). It checks whether the technical Ladder ID played contains the word specified in the filter.

docs.title: Practical Example:
docs.text:
If you create a quest with:

  • type: "WIN_RANKED"
  • typeFilter: "doubles"

The system will validate progress if the player wins on a ladder such as doubles_ranked or doubles_50_ranked, because both contain the keyword doubles. If the filter is configured as null or "", the mod ignores validation and accepts any ranked format.

docs.title: Matchmaking Queue Configuration
docs.text: The queue_config.json file controls the matchmaking rules for Cobblemon BattleHUB, focusing primarily on preventing result manipulation (Win Trading) through configurable cooldowns between the same players.

docs.title: File Path
docs.text: config/cobblemon\_battlehub/queue\_config.json

docs.title: Default Configuration Template
docs.text:
Below is the default JSON template automatically generated by the mod:

    {  
     "QueueAntiTradingCasual": 30,  
     "QueueAntiTradingRanked": 60  
    }

docs.title: 1. Win Trading Prevention (Casual Queue)
docs.text: • QueueAntiTradingCasual (Default: 30): The wait time (in minutes) that two specific players must wait before they can be matched again in the Casual queue after fighting each other. This prevents friends from abusing fast matchmaking to intentionally play consecutive matches in the public queue.

docs.title: 2. Win Trading Prevention (Ranked Queue)
docs.text: • QueueAntiTradingRanked (Default: 60): The wait time (in minutes) that two specific players must wait before they can be matched again in the Ranked queue after a competitive duel. Since ranked matches award rating points and rewards, this limit is higher (1 hour by default) to prevent artificial point gains (Elo boosting).

docs.title: How the Matchmaking System Works (Behind the Scenes)
docs.text: The Cobblemon BattleHUB algorithm performs several automatic safety checks when a player attempts to enter the queue:

docs.title: Penalty Check (Leaver Buster)
docs.text:
If a player tries to enter the Ranked queue but has a recent history of intentional disconnects (quits), the system blocks entry immediately:

  1. The mod checks whether the timeout for the desertion ban is still active.
  2. If it is active, the player receives a chat alert indicating the exact remaining suspension time formatted in hours and minutes.

docs.title: Rating Calculation (Glicko-2)
docs.text:
For competitive matches, the system does not perform random searches. It dynamically calculates:

  • Rating: The player’s current skill score (default starting value 1500.0).
  • RD (Rating Deviation): The reliability of the player’s skill estimate (default starting value 350.0). Matchmaking progressively expands acceptable Elo difference limits as queue wait time increases.

docs.title: Available Arena Validation
docs.text: The queue will only match players if there is at least one empty physical arena available in the battle dimension. If all arenas are occupied, players remain in queue waiting until a match finishes.

docs.title: How to Reload Changes
docs.text:
If you modify the anti-trading minute values in queue_config.json, apply the changes to the server in real time using the command:

/bh reload

docs.title: Ranks
docs.text:
Warning:

To view the formulas, set your page to Light Mode!

docs.title: Ranks and Seasons System
docs.text: Cobblemon BattleHUB has a sophisticated integrated competitive system for ranking and pairing players. This system ensures matches are fair, discourages intentional abandonment, and automates season transitions.

docs.title: The Rating Algorithm (Glicko-2)
docs.text:
Unlike the classic Elo system (which uses only a single fixed numerical score), BattleHUB uses the Glicko-2 algorithm to calculate players’ true skill with extreme precision.

The system works with two main variables stored in the database for each category (Ladder):

  1. Rating (): The player’s estimated skill score (starting default: ).
  2. Rating Deviation (): The rating deviation, which measures how uncertain the system is about the player’s skill level (starting default: ).

docs.title: How Does Uncertainty () Affect Matches?
docs.text:
The system is 95% confident the player’s true skill lies within the interval:

  • New/Inactive Players ( High): If your is high (close to ), the system is unsure about your level. Each win or loss will cause big Rating () swings to adjust your profile quickly.
  • Frequent Players ( Low): As you play regular matches, your decreases (approaching the safety limit, such as ). The system becomes more confident in your skill, and your Rating () changes become smaller and more stable.

docs.title: Scale Conversion
docs.text:
For the internal mathematical calculations of variance and volatility (), the system converts traditional Glicko values to the Glicko-2 scale using the multiplicative factor :

Where:

  • represents the rating mean on the Glicko-2 scale.
  • represents the rating deviation on the Glicko-2 scale.

docs.title: Desertion Penalty System (Leaver Buster)
docs.text: To prevent players from ruining the competitive experience by disconnecting from unfavorable battles, the CobblemonBattleHandler class monitors intentional abandons (Alt+F4, freezes, or crashes) and turn timeouts due to absence (AFK).

docs.title: What Happens in Case of Abandonment?
docs.text:
If a player disconnects or is punished for inactivity during a competitive match, the server triggers the recordForfeitOrAFK(loser) method:

  1. Automatic Loss Assignment: The player who abandoned receives an immediate loss and loses Rating () as normal.
  2. W.O. Victory for the Opponent: The active player receives a message informing them the opponent abandoned the match and gains the victory points.
  3. Temporary Ban (Leaver Ban): The deserter is prohibited from entering the competitive queue for a cumulative period.

When trying to re-enter the queue with an active ban, the player receives a dynamically formatted warning:

Warning Example: "You are banned from the competitive queue for another 01h 15m due to recent abandons."

docs.title: Profile Data History
docs.text:
All statistics are stored cleanly in the MySQL or SQLite database table. The monitored progress includes:

  • TOTAL_BATTLES: Number of matches played (Ranked + Casual).
  • RANKED_WINS: Total competitive wins.
  • RANKED_LOSSES: Total competitive losses.
  • RANKED_MATCHES: Total competitive matches completed.
  • QUICK_WINS and QUICK_MATCHES: Casual matchmaking stats.
  • RANKED_BAN_EXPIRATION: Timestamp in milliseconds indicating when the player may return to the ranked queue if penalized.

docs.title: Season Management
docs.text: The server administrator can automate seasons via console or in-game admin commands.

docs.title: Ending a Season (Season Rollover)
docs.text:
When you decide a season has ended, run the command:

/bh season rollover

This command automatically performs the following actions in the database:

  1. Determine Winners: Scans the rankings and generates Top 1, Top 3, and Top 10 rewards based on the commands configured in your server_config.json.
  2. Filter Inactive Players: Only players who met the minimumGames requirement for the season are eligible for rewards and included in the final rankings, preventing inactive accounts from holding top leaderboard positions.
  3. Archiving and Logging: The current season is saved into the historical data list (completedRankedSeasons).
  4. Reset Ranks (Soft Reset): The Rating () of all active players is pulled back toward the mean () and Rating Deviation () is reset to for the new season.
